entries.insight: Complexity is concentrated in the store layer (repository.ts, prep.ts, define.ts) — all three are accelerating hotspots. This cluster represents the core API surface and is the highest-risk area for regressions.
entries.context:
Confirmed across 6 snapshots. repository.ts: cyc 179, cog 124, 803 lines (25% of all source complexity alone). prep.ts: cyc 59, cog 46, 310L. define.ts: cyc 56, cog 34, 329L. Combined: 42% of all source complexity, all accelerating. Hotspot count rose from 29→34 across sessions. Recommend decomposing repository.ts before next CRUD feature work — extract soft-delete, upsert/conflict, or read/write split.
repository.ts still leads at 179 cyclomatic, 803 lines, 74% coverage — highest risk source file

entries.insight: Coverage data is intermittent. Earlier snapshot (04:49) captured 86.9% total coverage, but latest shows null. The snapshot pipeline's coverage collection is not reliably wired.
entries.context: Snapshot 2026-04-14T04:49 had totalCoverage: 86.9. Snapshots 2026-04-14T05:02 through 05:07 show null. Likely requires --coverage flag or vitest coverage config.

entries.insight: Test files account for a growing share of hotspots (now 10+ of 34). Test complexity is rising alongside source complexity — test helpers or shared fixtures could reduce this.
entries.context: Hotspot count rose 29→34. Test hotspots include withCache.test.ts (accelerating), withMembers.test.ts (accelerating), seed.test.ts (accelerating), commands.test.ts (accelerating).

entries.insight: connect.ts is the single highest-complexity file (86 cyc, 410L, hotspot score 77.8) but is cooling — active refactoring is paying off. Continue current trajectory.
entries.context:
Hotspot score 77.8 (highest) but trend is 'cooling'. 35 commits in 6mo. 44 tests cover it. Commands.ts (34.8) and bin/storium.ts (33.7) also cooling. While connect.ts has the highest raw hotspot score, repository.ts (accelerating, 179 cyc) is the more urgent concern.
connect.ts has highest hotspot score (77.8) but trend is cooling; coverage still low at 68%/64%

entries.insight: 100% of source files have test coverage mappings (18/18). No source file is untested. This is a strong foundation — the gap is coverage depth, not breadth.
entries.context: All 18 source files have coveredByTests entries. 393 tests tracked (up from 371). Confirmed in analysis cycle 2026-04-14.

entries.insight: migrate/commands.ts has lowest branch coverage of any source file (34%) with high churn (17 commits). CRAP score of 12 suggests untested complex paths.
entries.context:
60% line / 34% branch coverage, 40 cyclomatic, hotspot score 34.8 but cooling
migrate/commands.ts ranks #2 fragile: only 10 tests, 34% branch cov, no error-path or rollback tests

entries.insight: Five source files have accelerating hotspot trends simultaneously (repository, prep, define, zod, withMembers). All are core store-layer files — suggests active development pressure on the store subsystem.
entries.context: All accelerating files are in src/store/ or src/schema/ or src/mixins/

entries.insight: seed.ts has only 4 tests covering 251 lines (32 cyclomatic). No tests for error paths (missing directory, malformed seed files, partial failures). Ranks #4 fragile.
entries.context: Fragility score 2.75, 85% line but 60% branch coverage, hotspot cooling
